data.content:
### Enable Accessibility Layer for Charts

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Demonstrates how to add an accessibility layer to a chart component, such as `LineChart`, using the `accessibilityLayer` prop. This feature enhances keyboard navigation and screen reader support for the chart.

```tsx
<LineChart accessibilityLayer />
```

--------------------------------

### Define Chart Data for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet defines the `chartData` array, representing monthly desktop and mobile user statistics. It serves as the primary data source for rendering a Recharts bar chart, where each object contains a `month`, `desktop`, and `mobile` property.

```tsx
const chartData = [
  { month: "January", desktop: 186, mobile: 80 },
  { month: "February", desktop: 305, mobile: 200 },
  { month: "March", desktop: 237, mobile: 120 },
  { month: "April", desktop: 73, mobile: 190 },
  { month: "May", desktop: 209, mobile: 130 },
  { month: "June", desktop: 214, mobile: 140 },
]
```

--------------------------------

### Import XAxis for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet imports the `XAxis` component from the `recharts` library, which is used to display the horizontal axis of the chart. It's typically used to represent categories or time series data.

```tsx
import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid, XAxis } from "recharts"
```

--------------------------------

### Import CartesianGrid for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet imports the `Bar`, `BarChart`, and `CartesianGrid` components from the `recharts` library. `CartesianGrid` is necessary for adding grid lines to the chart's plotting area.

```tsx
import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid } from "recharts"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Recharts Bar Chart Labels and Colors (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet defines `chartConfig`, an object that holds human-readable labels and color tokens for the chart's data series, specifically 'desktop' and 'mobile'. It uses the `ChartConfig` type for type safety, allowing easy customization of chart appearance.

```tsx
import { type ChartConfig } from "@/components/ui/chart"

const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
    color: "#2563eb",
  },
  mobile: {
    label: "Mobile",
    color: "#60a5fa",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Implement a Basic Chart Component with Recharts and shadcn/ui

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This TypeScript React snippet demonstrates how to create a basic chart component using Recharts integrated with shadcn/ui's `ChartContainer` and `ChartTooltipContent`. It imports `Bar` and `BarChart` from Recharts and custom components from `@/components/ui/chart`. The `MyChart` function renders a bar chart with a tooltip, showcasing a compositional approach to chart building.

```tsx
import { Bar, BarChart } from "recharts"

import { ChartContainer, ChartTooltipContent } from "@/components/ui/chart"

export function MyChart() {
  return (
    <ChartContainer>
      <BarChart data={data}>
        <Bar dataKey="value" />
        <ChartTooltip content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
      </BarChart>
    </ChartContainer>
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import ChartTooltip for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet imports the custom `ChartTooltip` and `ChartTooltipContent` components from the `@/components/ui/chart` path. These components are designed to provide a rich, interactive tooltip experience for the chart.

```tsx
import { ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent } from "@/components/ui/chart"
```

--------------------------------

### Add CartesianGrid to Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet integrates the `CartesianGrid` component into the `BarChart` within a `ChartContainer`. Setting `vertical={false}` ensures only horizontal grid lines are displayed, enhancing readability without clutter.

```tsx
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig} className="min-h-[200px] w-full">
  <BarChart accessibilityLayer data={chartData}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" radius={4} />
    <Bar dataKey="mobile" fill="var(--color-mobile)" radius={4} />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Define a Chart Configuration Object in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Illustrates the structure of a `chartConfig` object for shadcn-ui charts, used to define labels, icons, and color themes for different data keys. This configuration is decoupled from the chart data, allowing for shared styling and flexible data integration.

```tsx
import { Monitor } from "lucide-react"

import { type ChartConfig } from "@/components/ui/chart"

const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
    icon: Monitor,
    // A color like 'hsl(220, 98%, 61%)' or 'var(--color-name)'
    color: "#2563eb",
    // OR a theme object with 'light' and 'dark' keys
    theme: {
      light: "#2563eb",
      dark: "#dc2626",
    },
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Add ChartTooltip to Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet integrates the `ChartTooltip` component into the `BarChart`, providing interactive data details on hover. It uses `ChartTooltipContent` to define the structure and appearance of the tooltip's content.

```tsx
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig} className="h-[200px] w-full">
  <BarChart accessibilityLayer data={chartData}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <XAxis
      dataKey="month"
      tickLine={false}
      tickMargin={10}
      axisLine={false}
      tickFormatter={(value) => value.slice(0, 3)}
    />
    <ChartTooltip content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
    <Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" radius={4} />
    <Bar dataKey="mobile" fill="var(--color-mobile)" radius={4} />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Add XAxis to Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This snippet adds and configures the `XAxis` component to the `BarChart`. It maps the `month` data key, removes tick lines and axis lines, adds margin, and formats the tick values to show only the first three characters of the month.

```tsx
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig} className="h-[200px] w-full">
  <BarChart accessibilityLayer data={chartData}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <XAxis
      dataKey="month"
      tickLine={false}
      tickMargin={10}
      axisLine={false}
      tickFormatter={(value) => value.slice(0, 3)}
    />
    <Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" radius={4} />
    <Bar dataKey="mobile" fill="var(--color-mobile)" radius={4} />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Integrate ChartTooltip into a shadcn-ui Chart (TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Illustrates adding the `ChartTooltip` component to a chart, utilizing `ChartTooltipContent` for its rendering. This setup enables interactive tooltips that appear on hover, enhancing user experience by providing context-sensitive data.

```tsx
<ChartTooltip content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Themed Colors within Chart Data (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Demonstrates how to embed CSS variable color references directly within the `chartData` array. This allows individual data points or series to inherit colors from the active theme, providing granular control over styling.

```tsx
const chartData = [
  { browser: "chrome", visitors: 275, fill: "var(--color-chrome)" },
  { browser: "safari", visitors: 200, fill: "var(--color-safari)" },
]
```

--------------------------------

### Apply CSS Variables to Chart Configuration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Demonstrates how to reference defined CSS variables (`--chart-1`, `--chart-2`) within the `chartConfig` object for color assignment. This method allows dynamic theming where chart colors adapt based on the active CSS theme (e.g., light/dark mode).

```tsx
const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
    color: "var(--chart-1)",
  },
  mobile: {
    label: "Mobile",
    color: "var(--chart-2)",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Define CSS Custom Properties for Chart Colors

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This CSS snippet defines custom properties for chart colors under the `@layer base` rule, supporting both light and dark themes. It provides a consistent color palette for charts, allowing for easy customization and integration into the application's overall styling. These variables are essential for the visual presentation of shadcn/ui charts.

```css
@layer base {
  :root {
    --chart-1: oklch(0.646 0.222 41.116);
    --chart-2: oklch(0.6 0.118 184.704);
    --chart-3: oklch(0.398 0.07 227.392);
    --chart-4: oklch(0.828 0.189 84.429);
    --chart-5: oklch(0.769 0.188 70.08);
  }

  .dark {
    --chart-1: oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376);
    --chart-2: oklch(0.696 0.17 162.48);
    --chart-3: oklch(0.769 0.188 70.08);
    --chart-4: oklch(0.627 0.265 303.9);
    --chart-5: oklch(0.645 0.246 16.439);
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Themed Colors to Chart Components (TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Illustrates applying a themed color to a `Bar` component using a CSS variable reference (`var(--color-KEY)`). This ensures the bar's fill color dynamically responds to the active theme defined in the chart configuration or CSS.

```tsx
<Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" />
```

--------------------------------

### Import and Use Chart Legend Components

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Shows the import statement for `ChartLegend` and `ChartLegendContent` components from the shad-cn/ui library. These components are used to render a legend for the chart.

```tsx
import { ChartLegend, ChartLegendContent } from "@/components/ui/chart"

<ChartLegend content={<ChartLegendContent />} />
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Themed Colors with Tailwind CSS (TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Shows how to use Tailwind CSS utility classes to apply themed colors to components like `LabelList`, referencing CSS variables directly. This approach integrates shadcn-ui's theming with Tailwind for consistent styling.

```tsx
<LabelList className="fill-[--color-desktop]" />
```

--------------------------------

### Integrate ChartLegend into a shadcn-ui BarChart (TSX)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Demonstrates how to add `ChartLegend` and `ChartLegendContent` within a `BarChart` component. This integration displays a legend, helping users understand the different data series represented in the chart, such as 'desktop' and 'mobile' data keys.

```tsx
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig} className="h-[200px] w-full">
  <BarChart accessibilityLayer data={chartData}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <XAxis
      dataKey="month"
      tickLine={false}
      tickMargin={10}
      axisLine={false}
      tickFormatter={(value) => value.slice(0, 3)}
    />
    <ChartTooltip content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
    <ChartLegend content={<ChartLegendContent />} />
    <Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" radius={4} />
    <Bar dataKey="mobile" fill="var(--color-mobile)" radius={4} />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Recharts Dependency Manually

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This bash command installs the `recharts` library using npm, which is a core dependency for shadcn/ui chart components. This step is crucial for the manual installation process, ensuring that all necessary charting functionalities are available in your project.

```bash
npm install recharts
```

--------------------------------

### Install shadcn/ui Chart Component via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

This bash command uses the `npx shadcn@latest add chart` utility to quickly install the `chart.tsx` component into your project. This is the recommended method for integrating shadcn/ui components, simplifying the setup process.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add chart
```

--------------------------------

### Directly Define Chart Colors in Configuration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Shows how to directly specify colors using hex, HSL, or OKLCH values within the `chartConfig` object. This approach offers flexibility for static color assignments without relying on CSS variables.

```tsx
const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
    color: "#2563eb",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Update Chart Configuration Color Definitions

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This diff shows how to update `chartConfig` by removing the `hsl()` wrapper from color definitions. This is possible because theme colors now include `hsl()` directly, streamlining color usage in chart configurations.

```diff
const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
-    color: "hsl(var(--chart-1))",
+    color: "var(--chart-1)",
  },
  mobile: {
    label: "Mobile",
-   color: "hsl(var(--chart-2))",
+   color: "var(--chart-2)",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Define CSS Variables for Chart Theming

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Defines custom CSS variables for chart colors within a global CSS file, supporting light and dark themes. These variables provide a centralized way to manage and apply color schemes consistently across shadcn-ui charts.

```css
@layer base {
  :root {
    --chart-1: oklch(0.646 0.222 41.116);
    --chart-2: oklch(0.6 0.118 184.704);
  }

  .dark: {
    --chart-1: oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376);
    --chart-2: oklch(0.696 0.17 162.48);
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import ChartLegend Components in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Imports the `ChartLegend` and `ChartLegendContent` components from the shadcn-ui chart library. These components are essential for adding a legend to a chart, providing context for the data series displayed.

```tsx
import { ChartLegend, ChartLegendContent } from "@/components/ui/chart"
```

--------------------------------

### Customize Chart Tooltip Labels and Names with labelKey and nameKey

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Demonstrates how to customize the tooltip's displayed label and names using `labelKey` and `nameKey` props. This allows referencing specific keys from chart data or configuration for dynamic display. Ensure `chartData` and `chartConfig` are correctly defined.

```tsx
const chartData = [
  { browser: "chrome", visitors: 187, fill: "var(--color-chrome)" },
  { browser: "safari", visitors: 200, fill: "var(--color-safari)" },
]

const chartConfig = {
  visitors: {
    label: "Total Visitors",
  },
  chrome: {
    label: "Chrome",
    color: "hsl(var(--chart-1))",
  },
  safari: {
    label: "Safari",
    color: "hsl(var(--chart-2))",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig

<ChartTooltip
  content={<ChartTooltipContent labelKey="visitors" nameKey="browser" />}
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Customize Chart Legend Names with nameKey

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx

Illustrates how to customize the names displayed in the chart legend using the `nameKey` prop on `ChartLegendContent`. This allows specifying which data key to use for legend item labels. The `chartData` and `chartConfig` should be properly set up.

```tsx
const chartData = [
  { browser: "chrome", visitors: 187, fill: "var(--color-chrome)" },
  { browser: "safari", visitors: 200, fill: "var(--color-safari)" },
]

const chartConfig = {
  chrome: {
    label: "Chrome",
    color: "hsl(var(--chart-1))",
  },
  safari: {
    label: "Safari",
    color: "hsl(var(--chart-2))",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig

<ChartLegend content={<ChartLegendContent nameKey="browser" />} />
```
